chief_complaint: A 50-year-old woman presented with progressive, left-sided pleuritic chest pain.
history: The patient experienced progressive, left-sided pleuritic chest pain. Initial investigations were negative. On re-presentation, inflammatory markers were raised and a small left pleural effusion was noted.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG: negative, Troponin: negative, D-dimer: negative, Inflammatory markers: raised, CT pulmonary angiography: focal pericardial fat inflammation.
